title: hoya fauziana subsp. angulata
intro: Hoya fauziana subsp. angulata, also known as a woody climber, is a subspecies of the Hoya fauziana plant, a type of flowering succulent native to the tropical regions of Southeast Asia. The plant has been found to have medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to exhibit antibacterial and anti-inflammatory activities, which contribute to its potential health benefits, such as wound healing and the reduction of inflammation. The herbal preparations of Hoya fauziana subsp. angulata typically involve the use of its leaves or stems, which can be made into teas, infusions, or extracts to harness its medicinal properties. Further research is needed to fully understand the health benefits and optimal uses of Hoya fauziana subsp. angulata, but preliminary findings suggest its potential in traditional medicine.
